Title: Squeaky Nightmare – Beware of These Shoes!
Text: I had high hopes for these shoes, but unfortunately, they turned out to be one of the most annoying pairs I’ve ever owned. Right out of the box, they looked great—sleek design, comfortable fit—but the moment I started walking, the nightmare began.  These shoes squeak with every single step, no matter what surface I’m on. Tile? Squeak. Hardwood? Squeak. Even on carpet, there’s still a faint noise. It’s like having a built-in sound effect following me everywhere. I’ve tried everything—adjusting the laces, checking the insoles, even applying powder to the soles—but nothing stops the relentless noise.  What’s worse, the squeaking isn’t just a minor annoyance—it’s LOUD. I wore them to work once, and people kept turning to see where the sound was coming from. Walking through a quiet hallway or a library? Forget it. You might as well announce your arrival from a mile away.  Comfort-wise, they’re decent, but honestly, that doesn’t matter when I feel like I’m starring in my own personal sound effects reel. Unless you enjoy the embarrassment of sounding like a human rubber duck, I strongly recommend avoiding these shoes.

Title: Pegasus Premium is finally here! But it's not affordable.
Text: I've been waiting a whole year for this shoe since I first heard about it in 2024!  Sculpted air unit is just too cool!  This shoe looks awesome, and it is very cushiony on foot.  It is very responsive and comfortable.  It is not a lightweight shoe, but it's also not that heavy.  I think Nike should make the tongue less bulky in future iterations.  It works as a daily running, walking, or lifestyle shoe, but I wouldn't choose this for race day.  Unless you like a snug fit, I recommend going a half size up from your usual Pegasus or Nike running shoe size.  I have the Pegasus 41 and Pegasus Plus, and this shoe is definitely more snug in the toe box and does not allow for toe splay if I don't size up.  This shoe is definitely more responsive than the Pegasus Plus.  The upper is a light grey color, not white as how it may appear in the picture, but it still looks awesome!  Nike - please make this shoe a little more affordable.  While it's a very cool shoe, I don't know if I really need it for this price point amidst all my other running shoes.

Title: Awesome recovery shoe.
Text: I have really enjoyed running in the Nike Vomero Plus. The cushioning is the standout feature. It feels plush and protective without being unstable, which makes a big difference on longer runs and recovery days. My legs feel noticeably fresher after higher mileage sessions.  The ride is smooth and consistent, especially at easy to steady paces. Heel to toe transitions feel controlled, and the shoe gives me confidence on pavement when I am settling into a rhythm. The upper is comfortable and breathable, with enough room in the forefoot to stay comfortable as miles add up.  This is not a speed focused shoe, and it does feel heavier when I try to push the pace. For tempo runs or faster efforts, I would reach for something lighter. But for daily training, long runs, and steady aerobic work, it does exactly what I want.  Overall, the Vomero Plus is a dependable, comfort driven running shoe that excels at protecting my legs and keeping runs enjoyable. It is a great choice for runners who value cushioning, stability, and consistency over pure speed.

Title: Great Max Cushion Shoe
Text: Preparing for a marathon and needed a new pair of shoes for my long easy runs. I was a BIG fan of the ZoomX Invincible 1, a slightly lesser fan of the ZI2, and didn’t like the ZI3 at all (not a bad shoe, just wasn’t for me). I switched to Brooks for my last marathon and they didn’t hold up well, which put me back in the market. When I read about the Vomero Plus picking up where the Invincible line left off, I was intrigued. I have to say, 126 miles later, this is an awesome max cushion shoe. Combines the best elements of the Invincible line with key improvements, particularly the upper and the outsole. Also true to size (whereas I used to order 1/2 size up). My legs and feet feel incredible after long slow runs and even picking up speed due to the surprising energy return for a max cushion. I am definitely buying another pair when these retire, hopefully around 300 miles. FYI I rotate the Zoom Fly 6 for shorter, faster runs and it seems to be a good combination. I’m not a competitive runner so I didn’t need to buy the more expensive models.

Title: Finished 1st 10K Run!
Text: I'm 5'10, around 197 pounds, and I'm in my forties. And I have been training as a runner for a couple of years now. For most of this year, I have been using the Pegasus 41 for daily runs, speed runs, and 5K runs. I also have the Vaporfly 3, which I use exclusively in organized racing. While I am pleased with those shoes in most situations, I felt like I needed a shoe that will help me run longer and farther. And I don't want to wear out the Vaporflys too early. The Vomero Plus is the shoe that helps me in those regards. I was able to have an uninterrupted run for about 80 minutes. And I was able to run for 10,000 meters for the first time. Mind you, the shoe doesn't feel particularly fast. However, it took longer for my feet to get tired while wearing the Vomero Plus, thanks to the generous amount of ZoomX cushioning. I'm quite sure that I had more fun while wearing the Vomero Plus. I just threw on my headphones and ran while listening to a 60-minute podcast episode. Also, this shoe is a little wider in the midfoot than the Peg 41. At the same time, my heel felt pretty stable. And I appreciate how there is more rubber on the outsole than the Peg 41, so the grip is nice. I plan on rotating all three shoes for different goals. But as of today, the Vomero Plus just could be one of my favorite running shoes of all time!
Very comfortable, I use them for work shoes and they been working so far

Title: Return to form!
Text: Writing this review after 6 weeks and 75 miles.  First the good.  This is a fantastic training shoe.  I've taken these out mostly for long/easy runs between five and 14 miles.  The cushioning is superb, I've had no stability issues, and only one hot spot that never occurred again so probably a sock problem.  They also cope with picking up the pace, and respond well to tempo/half marathon pace intervals. Fit true to size, with good room in the toe box.  And the bad.  I've taken a star off as the sole is already showing signs of serious wear on the heel after only 75 miles running on flat pavement/road, so don't have much hope of getting to 250.  Which is a real shame, because these have become my go to easy/recovery shoe after eight years of Asics Nimbus.  Same again for the Plus 2 next year with a more durable sole, please Nike!

Title: Comfortable shoe to build miles during the week and long runs
Text: I’ve run about 90 miles in these over the last 18 days and I would describe the difference in feel, compared to running in Nike Frees (which I also love) being like going from sleeping on a pad on the floor to sleeping on a memory foam mattress. I’m increasing mileage now as I build up for my next marathon and also wanted something that would help alleviate tension around both Achilles and switching to these shoes seems to help with that. These feel like they are going to help run a ton of miles without wearing down. They do have a little bit of a bounce/wobble to them, but I ultimately like this. I also like it that they don’t have a flyplate, it’s great to have this cushioning without that same amount of forward propulsion for training. One thing to note, the offset is 10mm and I normally run in 8mm or less. I was hesitant to get something with a greater heel to toe drop because I tried that once and it felt like running with a door jam under foot, like blocking my heel from dropping normally. Thankfully, these squish enough when you land that the extra 2mm compared to what I’m used to is not noticeable at all. Finally, while I like these a lot, I would be very curious to try these with more of a Flyknit upper because the upper does feel like a bit of extra cushion and it takes some attention to get the laces tied up tight so that they don’t feel too big or loose. I just saw the overview of the Vomero Premium before I wrote this, and it looks like the new option is a little bit trimmed down in the upper, which looks like it will be great.

Title: Vomero's the best cousin to Invincible.
Text: My Vomero arrived today, and I started my run. I've put in 3.70 miles on it. These running shoes have the bounce and the cushion to do a long, slow run. I know 5km running, dude, and I could go long. I will use these Vomeros as my long-running shoes.   Here's the gist of the Vomero: it's stable, not too heavy, comfortable, and grippy. The ZoomX midsole enables you to push forward, and it's soft on every landing. There is no harshness on landing and nary an instability.    I ought to run at Golden Gate Park with this pair. I hope to push that 5-mile barrier. My goal is to reach 10 miles and keep it consistent.   Does it have any faults or flaws? The laces are too short for me to use the heel lock/runner's loop. I wish the laces were a little longer so I could do that lacing. So, just one more thing that Nike needs to work on is all their running shoes, giving them a roomier toe box.

Title: Plush Cushioning with Surprising Stability – Great for Long Miles
Text: I picked up the Vomero Plus for daily road runs, and they’ve quickly become my go‑to. The fit runs true to size in my regular width, and even in the extra‑wide option, the lockdown feels secure without pinching. Despite the tall cushioning stack, they feel stable — I never got the “wobbly” sensation I’ve had with other max‑cushion shoes. The ZoomX midsole is the star here — ultra‑soft underfoot yet noticeably springy, which makes longer runs and recovery days a lot easier on my legs. The engineered mesh upper breathes well and wraps the foot comfortably, while the padding around the collar adds a premium feel. Style‑wise, the bold stack gives a chunkier profile, so if you prefer a super‑sleek look, this may not be your pick, but I like the mix of performance and standout design. At $180, it’s an investment, but the comfort, durability, and versatile ride make it a solid value if you log lots of miles.

Title: Bouncy but super light! Pure comfort, pure joy!

After having gone without a pair of everyday shoes long enough, absolutely no doubt about it, it was time for me to get another pair of shoes. Upon purchasing my very first pair of Nike Air Force 1, a classic, all-white (low), I had finally realized all of the reasons that this sneaker is still one of the most popular shoes available today. This review will evaluate the AF1 of its Design, Comfort, Durability, Versatility and Overall Value so that I can provide an honest opinion on how these shoes are for everyday wear. The Air Force 1 was the first ever basketball shoe to feature Nike Air technology, revolutionizing the game and sneaker culture forever. Over three decades since its first release, the Air Force 1 remains true to its roots while earning its status as a fashion staple for seasons to come. I ordered the shoes straight from the Nike app and they had come in surprisingly quick. The design of the box wasn't very special, but it was classic and stood out. The first look of the Air Force 1 has a pure white look that grabs your attention. The shoe is smooth leather and has subtle Nike swoosh. The all white look gives that sense of cleanliness of the shoes. The shoe cuts just below the ankle gives a good shape that works well with many of my outfits. What I like most is how the shoe is stylish without being super showy. It’s a shoe I have been able to wear in school, going out, or even more dressed-up events.  My first experience with putting on my pair of AF1s was that they were way more comfortable than I expected. I had heard a lot of reviews about this shoe from people before I bought them, I believe they are a pretty comfortable shoe. There is definitely a lot of cushioning under your foot that is soft while you walk in the shoe. Although the shoe is a little heavier than your typical sneaker. If you haven't ever put your foot through a new pair of shoes for several hours, you will discover that just after sitting for a while, they will start to feel just a little bit weird, but after about a full day of wearing them, they will start to fit naturally on your feet and will not cause any issues when worn for long periods of time. The comfort of the shoe is essential to everyday sneaker wear. If you plan to be wearing AF1s regularly, you will certainly enjoy the comfort. One of the reasons I think the cost is justified is because of the durability of these shoes. The leather resists quick wear-and-tear and the creases do develop at the top of the shoes. The creases can be helped by buying a crease protection which I had to do. After wearing my shoes for about a month I have still been able to keep them clean. The laces are pretty easy to keep clean but dust and dirt are a great attraction to them.  Overall, the Nike Air Force 1 has definitely earned a place in my regular rotation of shoes. I am definitely glad I decided to finally get AF1's. Its classic design goes great with a lot of my outfits. They also have been a shoe I can use for nice events or even just dinners.

Title: I’m able to walk for the first time in over two years. I asked God for healing and He gave me a pair of shoes, Nike Motiva
Text: I broke every ankle bone and suffered severe dislocation in my right foot, May 2023. They inserted a 12 inch nail from the bottom of my heel to the top of my tibia. I had severe pain with the nail, so they removed it 2 days before Thanksgiving, 2024. I just healed from the incisions and bruising a couple of months ago. I live on a fixed income, so I couldn’t buy shoes until my birthday in May. I had one pair of shoes, in which I only used the left shoe with a walking boot. I bought a pair of Women’s Nike Motiva. I normally wear an 8.5. The website suggested a half size larger, since Nike runs small. I chose a 9.5, since my toes need more room and I need to account for a larger right foot with the bone calcification. I put them on and stood up to take my first step in shoes, in over two years. I don’t have a right calf muscle, due to muscle atrophy, so I have to walk carefully. I could not believe it. I was walking on a CUSHION in shoes for the first time since my ankle break. I don’t have a limp, weird gait or pain. I have more range of motion in these shoes. I look and feel like a normal person. I was in tears… I did not think I could ever take a step without pain, much less without a limp or a gait that was pronounced. I can push the accelerator on the car with my shoes on. I wasn’t able to do that before. The deep cushion on the bottom of the shoes alleviates pain and pressure when I need more range of motion. I asked God for healing and He gave me a pair of shoes, Nike Motiva.

Most of my time walking/running has been on softer rubber or smooth tile surfaces, so there is absolutely no excuse for this. My experience with the durability has genuinely turned me off of Nike shoes fully, and unless the tread durability is addressed soon, I don't plan on coming back to Nike shoes. Im giving 3 stars for the fact that the rest of the shoe was great, but if there were a rating for durability, I would only give it one star.

Title: Get this Motiva shoe!! Trust me..
Text: 10/10! RECOMMEND! I am so loving this Motiva shoe. Take the time to read the Nike info and reviews because they are very accurate. I usually wear a 10 but I got a half size up. For me personally I would go up an entire size because I do wear thick socks BUT the fit on these are fantastic! When I put the shoes on, it feels like my heel is resting inside a cup. Your arch is very supported. I have yet to find a shoe that has this type of support. If you are short like me, you will definitely feel the lift. If you are a thick and chunky yet funky type of chick like me, you will feel the difference in your knees and legs. When I started to walk and the way the soles are made, the feeling in my strides was weird. Nike said they propel you forward and they are right but after a few steps while getting used to that feeling, it go time! It feels like you are floating on clouds with this shoe. Very comfortable and cute! If I could I would buy every color.

LOVE THEM!!!  A perfect fit. Wore then for Tacky Light Run in the 34 degree and my feet felt sooooooo good during the walk. Glad they arrived just in time for the walk.  Now my daughter want a pair and I will need to get another pair before the 10K walk.

At this price point, I had hoped they would last me much longer, but I would order them again if I needed a pair of casual walking shoes.

The reason I'm abandoning my former shoes is that the soles started falling apart in a very short period of time. I'm reserving a star until I know how long these shoes will last.

I would recommend these to anyone who walks for fun or has a job that requires they be on their feet for a long time. Very strong arch support, bouncy, soft, flexible, and I have had no blisters or sore spots. The ONLY two downsides are that they tend to get warm and run a little snug. Title: Absolutely amazing engineering.
Text: I want to preface this by saying that I usually do not write reviews unless someone asks. I am writing this review because this is my all-time favorite shoe and I buy a lot of shoes. I own the Motiva and the Nike Invincible 3. I love them both. They feel similar, like walking on a cloud or a marshmallow. I use this shoe for walking mostly. I average 10,000 steps per day. I used them in Tokyo for 4 days of seemingly non-stop walking. I averaged about 22,000 steps per day. My feet and my legs were not tired the next day, but I did wear them with compression socks. No pain felt anywhere else. I would buy these shoes again without question. I hope they make more colorways for this shoe. I wear an 11.5 and I found them to be true to size. Great breathability.

The emphasis is the rocker and cushioned heel makes it like walking on cloud - and they don’t have the same support / restriction that running trainers do. So this makes them more comfortable and breathable.   Not sure how they’d hold up off-road, as they are great looking too!

Title: The invincible threes are softer and more pleasure cushiony than the Nike Motiva

The INVINCIBLE RUN 3 is more stable and they don’t have the rocker effect like Nike Motiva.   Why am I posting this review as I called customer service there was a lot of debate. No one was able to answer this question so I thought I’d add this onto the website review so people could know the plush Ness and cushiness is still going to the Nike invincible run 3. so if you’re looking for the most cushion, shoe at the moment the zoom foam, which is on the invincible run 3 is still the softer pillow feel and definitely the most softest and comfort over any other shoe that Nike has so far to date   I hope this helps others when trying to compare for the must plushy comfort

Title: I love them so much!!

When I first put them on I was shocked!! I’m not used to a shoe that is basically shaped like the bottom of a row boat - but I LOVE IT!! It gives my foot the perfect rocking motion when walking and gives me proper walking form! My knees and back have never felt better!! In fact, I love them so much that I’m buying ANOTHER pair!! If you’re on the fence about getting these - don’t be! Get them before they sell out in the color you want!!

Title: great WALKING shoe that runs small
Text: Ordered the Volt in an 8 from Nordstrom. The shoe I received is the neon yellow color shown on Nike’s site and not the neon green with black shoelace holes shown on the Nordstrom site.   Sizing is off. I’m a true 8, my foot measures an 8 on the shoe ruler, & all of my Nike’s are 8s. THIS shoe?  My big toe hits the edge but more importantly, the arch of the shoe sits behind the arch of my foot making this shoe uncomfortable and the wrong size.   Since Nike launched they’ve rarely made a WALKING shoe; design & feature wise they hit it out of the park with this shoe. The rocker sole is fantastic. The insole is soft & squishy & the color ways are fun. I’m ordering the white in a 8.5 for comparison before exchanging the Volt color I have.   This WALKING shoe should not be used or mistaken for a running shoe. Buy the right shoe for the task to minimize injury. Runners are misusing this shoe & leaving bad reviews for their mistakes. NIKE really needs to expand their walking shoe options bc I am here for them.

Title: Just Do It (Comfort you need)
Text: The comfort I need. This is truly a walking shoe, nothing less, nothing more. Nike has mastered the craft of creating exactly what everyone who wants to Just Do It. Great value for your money.

Title: So comfy! Laces suck
Text: 5 stars because despite having to re-tie my laces every 10 mins it is such a comfy shoe. I can walk so long and fast in these. I don’t like double knotting my laces because I’m picky and often re-tie them until my feet are as comfortable as possible, but I’ve been needing to with these shoes because the laces are too silky and constantly untie. My feet also cramp A LOT in these shoes if the laces aren’t adjusted perfectly. Not sure why. But once the laces are situated it’s truly such a comfortable walking shoe. Super cushioned and great for my arch.

Title: Great Shoe, But.....
Text: Super comfortable shoe and awesome to walk in. I personally would not run in these, but I'm not able to run so no biggie. The only thing stopping this from being a 5 star review is the toe box. Sorry Nike, the toe box is not wide or even natural at all. Your best toe box is on the footscape woven and that's the toe box that should have been used. Anyway, I still love the shoes and I'm definitely getting another pair.

I usually have 2 or more pairs of sneakers with me in my car to change out of the morning pair during my lunch. These are the only pair that I didn't have to "break in", or put an insert inside of them. They were instantly comfortable, and first day I forgot that I had them on, because I normally change my shoes during my lunch break. Title: Ultraboosts w/o the overengineering. Better imo
Text: I'm 6'3/205 lbs with medium arches, wide feet, and around 12.5 shoe size.  I see these shoes basically as a budget ultraboost, a happy medium between the 1.0 and the light. Not as comfortable as the ultraboost 1.0 but more structurally sound. Not as structurally sound as the ultraboost light but more comfortable.  The outsole is not as "cushy" as the ultraboost, thus you experience more ground-feel. However, as someone who is extremely active and on my feet most of the day, this is what you want in the long run. Overly cushy soles lead to weak feet and are bad for your knees.  So if you want the ultraboost look but without the costly overengineering, then I highly recommend these.

Title: Lived up to the hype
Text: I recently purchased the Adidas Pureboost shoes, and I couldn't be happier with my decision. As someone who wears a size US 12 with wide feet and spends 9 hours a day on my feet, these shoes have been a game-changer. The cushioning is exceptional, providing comfort and support throughout long shifts, and the wide fit accommodates my feet perfectly. Despite walking 200 miles a month at work, these shoes have held up incredibly well, showing no signs of wear and tear. The stylish design is an added bonus, making me feel confident both on and off the clock. Overall, I highly recommend the Adidas Pureboost for anyone seeking comfort, durability, and style in their footwear.

Designed with durability and comfort in mind, they provide excellent support during my gym sessions, offering a snug fit that enhances stability without sacrificing flexibility. The cushioning is top-notch, absorbing impact effectively and ensuring my feet feel less fatigued even after prolonged use. Additionally, the design is stylish yet functional, with breathable materials that keep my feet cool and dry. Whether lifting weights or doing cardio, these shoes deliver outstanding performance, making them a fantastic choice for anyone serious about their fitness regimen.

Title: Consider to go half size up (not more)!
Text: Fits snug like a glove. But I have tested with office thin socks (walking only). In comparison with Duramo 10, the upper part of the shoe is snug. In my opinion the sole a little shorter vs D10. So, if Duramo 9 and 10 models , size US10 was a little roomy (even with sport socks), this one fits snug. There is a little room for my fingers, however, this is won't enough while running. So, go half size up.   Due to 8mm drop, it's comfy for walking. Esp when you walk fast and hit the pavement with the toe. I think 2 separate EVA used to build a sole.   All Duramos need a break-in period. So this one either. First feeling can be that shoes are stiff, but later it becomes softer and softer, esp when it's hot. Duramo stays as a best daily trainer for a descent price.   Great job Adidas.

In addition, the material was also not what I expected, with the top being made from a shiny and slightly inflexible type of fabric, similar to something you'd see on a waterproof coat. Title: Never again, AVOID!!!!
Text: Product oversized. Returned to request smaller size, they returned the same pair back to me with a vague note saying they had failed a returns test.  No detail given at all.  After 3 online chats and a phonecall I was told that they were classed as soiled and worn...........  Strannge thing is, I returned 2 pairs of trainers, both tried on under the same conditions..................  Customer service is a total disgrace, shocking communications (lack of) and rude. Title: Very poor quality
Text: I bought a pair of Adidas SL 72’s in the London store a few weeks ago. Initially loved the look, however they made a strange, squeaky noise which I thought was due to the fact that they were new. The noise wasn’t particularly an issue as I liked the look and feel of them. Fast forward less than a month and the sole on one of them is coming away. Furthermore, one of the stripes on the side is also coming away, so really disappointing quality. What was worse was the attitude of Adidas staff when I tried to get the matter resolved. I was referred to WhatsApp and the response to my complaint was to contact the store. I did this, but the lady on the phone insisted that I return them to the London store. I live a good 2 hours from London and it would cost me the same in the value of the shoes getting there. After I tried explaining this, the woman didn’t listen and clearly couldn’t care less. I am therefore left over 90 pounds out of pocket with a pair of trainers which are less than a month old and falling apart. I am a 51 year old man and all I have done in them is walk; certainly no hard yards to make them fall apart. Disgusting quality and frankly disgusting customer service from a company who clearly couldn’t care less. I shall take my business elsewhere in future!

My partner is usually an 11.5 and the light blues were way too big on him. If a color you really want is on the verge of selling out I'd order a couple sizes to make sure you get a pair that fits.

Title: Nice color comfortable shoe but size is larger.
Text: I recently found myself in an unusual situation. I ordered this shoe in White, but after receiving it one shoe ( right foot ) was larger than the other. I was hopeful that this would be a straightforward process in exchange it but however, turned out to be a mixed experience, having to order a different color because the white was out of stock, so I exchanged it for a different color, however the execution fell short in a few areas when the shoe arrive I was excited to try them on only to find that it was still not quite right the same problem exist the same with the right foot being larger than the left, including some visible flaws with the material on both shoes as Some brushing up the suede, not being smooth as supposed. This is very disappointing because this was the problem I had in returning the previous order. Hoping that once I return this order, this will be corrected. the  I really was expecting better. Maybe this is a factory defect, but I’am not sure or satisfied with the look. I will be calling customer service. Mail orders just ain’t what I expected,  I think they are just pushing mixed match shoes off on my order. I need answers,  this is unexceptionable for such a quality product to be so inconsistent and so many flaws with their product being mixed match.

Text: This review is both about the quality of the shoes and the pathetic customer service.   I received this shoe and it had a mark on it. I had to engage in long chats with the customer service for 10 days to get the shoe exchanged. I was told there was some technical issue which was being looked into. Somehow, one of them was able to generate a return label for me.  I received my replacement shoe and this one also had material defect (unnecessary and bad stitch quality) and I am being asked by the customer care that rather an exchanging the shoe, I return it, get a refund and order a new one. The technical issue still persists.  I don't understand what are the quality standards being maintained as I have received a faulty shoe twice. Secondly, the customer care raises the issue to seniors in the team but I have never received a solution.  Please keep this review in mind while ordering coz just in case you receive a faulty shoe, you will be spending time with the customer care.

Title: Too long, narrow, lightweight, perfect for summer.
Text: I'd purchased a pair in a beautiful Blue / Off White / Clear Sky colorway in size 7.5, but they were too long for me, the back of the heels were slipping. Then I got the ones in Blue / Bright Red / Royal Blue in size 7, and they were too tight in a toebox for my liking since they do run narrow. I guess I'm "in between" sizes to be able to feel comfy in men's SL 72. Pitty. For the reference I'm size 9 in women's, and always purchase men's in 7.5, with an exception of Sambas in size 8. If you have narrow feet, size 7 will probably fit you if you wear a size 9 in women's. I'll wait for more options in women's colorways for SL 72 and try to get them in my regular size.

Title: Incorrect sizing guide
Text: The shoes are fine but not my style. The biggest issue is that this is the second time that I'm trying to order from the website and am getting enormous shoes. First time was maybe 6 years ago and I ended up returning the shoes unsure what size I should be getting. So I wanted to give this brand a try again. I cannot believe that ADIDAS has not corrected their sizing guide. And that shoes are listed as "men" when in reality they are unisex. I bought the 7.5 "men" size which should be 8.5 in women's based on the sizing guide and I received shoes that feel like they are 3 sizes too large. Of course, I cannot keep buying different sizes until I figure out the correct sizing. I am amazed that ADIDAS has not corrected this.  This is a potential loss in sales for the company for such an easy fix.

Title: Comfortable, stylish and very well made.
Text: I love them! They are so comfortable and well made that this is my second pair. Bought them in green and blue. They offer plenty of support for everyday wear as well as running if that’s what you want to use them for. Their retro style fits right in with modern looks. The only gripe I can come up with is the way the tongue tends to fold over inside when you’re putting them on so you have to reach in and rearrange it for a comfortable feel.  It’s a minor complaint and I’m not sure what the solution would be.  Overall these shoes are a great product that I would recommend to anyone who is a fan of adidas classic looks.  Additionally, while I personally spent a lot more on my adidas running shoes and am very happy with them, these shoes would definitely provide the kind of support and construction necessary for. Quality running shoe at a very reasonable price compared to more expensive specialty running foot wear.

Title: Great trainer with great discount
Text: I purchased this product as it appealed to me by the colours - mainly my favourite - and was on offer with a nice big % off, so much so that I allowed myself the indulgence of personalisation which was an added feature. The customisation process was easy and accurate and being able to choose emojis too (although a small range) was fun. The ordering and payment process was smooth, email communications too. The product was delivered on time to the store I selected, and packaging was sturdy and undamaged. I would add that I questioned if the outer box that the trainers box was inside was really necessary though, thinking about material waste. The trainers inside were fresh, quality check found no faults and custom personalities printing on the sides was well done and smart. Once tried on the foot fit was a bit tight and a squeeze, however at the end of a day out and about wearing them the comfort prevailed and I was satisfied they would be comfortable going forward. Overall I am very happy with this product. Thanks Adidas!

Text: The Adidas Duramo 5 is a solid choice if you're just getting into running or want something comfortable for the gym and everyday wear. The support is fine for casual use, but if you’re running serious miles or need strong arch support, you might find it lacking. They’re great value for the price, though the sole can wear faster on rough surfaces

Title: Comfortable, cushy, good quality
Text: Really cushy and comfortable, the top of the shoe hugs your foot to keep an overall a slim profile and the sole flares out laterally to add stability. This is great for someone like me who has a glass ankle and occasionally rolls it. I previously wore the Duramo model and I think this is a better choice for me, using it for extremely fast walking around London. The one aspect I would change is the quality around the final eyelet holes when you're lacing it up--the edge has an unfinished look and it looks kinda chintzy, detracting from the value of the shoe. But functionally it performs well and is really comfortable, and if anyone were to look at these on your feet, they wouldn't see the unfinished detail.

Title: Too good to be true!
Text: The adidas NY 90 shoes bring a nostalgic, retro-inspired design with a modern twist, making them a standout in the brand’s lineup. Inspired by the street culture and fashion scene of the ‘90s, these sneakers feature a combination of premium leather and suede for a stylish yet durable construction. The upper is complemented by adidas’ signature three-stripes, adding a touch of authenticity and heritage. The cushioned EVA midsole ensures comfort and support, making them suitable for daily wear or casual outings. With a sleek silhouette, the NY 90 shoes are versatile enough to pair with jeans, shorts, or joggers, easily blending into any casual or streetwear look. The rubber outsole provides reliable grip and stability, while the overall design remains timeless, evoking the spirit of ‘90s New York fashion. Whether you’re an adidas fan or simply looking for a comfortable, stylish sneaker, the NY 90 offers a perfect balance of vintage appeal and contemporary performance.

Fit

I’m never a size 7 in US. I even sometimes order size 8 in other brands. Honestly, a 7.5 in women’s would work too! Get these shoes! They look so great on! Title: Good but design might need work.
Text: I love the style and the look and so far they are comfy, besides one aspect.  I bought these for getting back into running at the gym, but the heel part comes up way higher than other shoes. This piece is uncomfortable and digs in when walking or running. Now I do have a little extra weight on me, so maybe it digs in more than other people who might have smaller ankles, but it is an annoying part of the shoe and if I don't get taller socks, I'm thinking I'll start to get skin rubbed off. Other than that, I do like this shoe, just not seeming to be the best for what it's advertised for.

Title: Comfy and bright!
Text: For many years I was a size US 7.5 women’s in Adidas no matter what, but lately the sizes sometimes seem a little larger than normal & not as predictable - so I ordered both a 7 and 7.5 to try. The 7.5 was too big and the 7 perfect, so a half size down for me. I have a bunion on the left foot on big toe side so I always have to consider the material and width/room so it doesn’t rub when I’m working out. These seem comfy but haven’t worked out in them yet so we’ll see. The shoe material is bright and flexible. I use these type of shoes Cardio Kickbox, Zumba etc and like the thicker bottoms of some of these running shoes to take the impact. I have knee issues as well so comfort is a big plus. And they are a nice hot pink - it has been quite a while since Adidas has made a bright pink shoe in a style I like (I’ve been looking for a few years!) so glad when I saw these during the sale. I’ve been wearing Adidas loyally ever since I was a kid in the 80’s & saw Run DMC wear/rap about them!

Title: NEVER RECIEVED THE SHOES
Text: I recently purchased my first pair of shoes from Adidas, which were delivered through Amazon Prime. Unfortunately, the shoes never arrived. I submitted a request to Adidas, and they assured me they would investigate. After 48 hours, I was told to check with Amazon. When I contacted Amazon, they informed me that since the order wasn't placed through them, I needed to speak with Adidas.  I have called both companies multiple times and have gotten nowhere. Despite filing a police report, I have received no assistance from either company. I am incredibly upset and plan to never buy anything from either website again. I would have been satisfied with another pair of shoes, store credit, or even just someone who genuinely cared about resolving my issue.

Title: Great shoe and versatile for short runs
Text: Comfy and feels like running on clouds. The shoe allows you to increase speed without feeling like your effort is 10:10

Title: Great product for the price point.
Text: The comfort and simplicity of the shoes makes it great for daily runs. The price made it even better with its affordability.

Title: Chose this as a gear to start with jogging/running
Text: Very comfortable and design is very simple. I already did total of 20km with it and my foot never hurt. Well recommended considering the price.

Title: I have the white ones and now have the black ones.
Text: love them. so comfy. bought the white ones too. so light, it's like wearing super comfy nothing on your feet

Title: 100% going to be shoe of the year 2025.
Text: Super comfortable, super light and fun to run in. I will be using this for the majority of my training and I will 100% be getting more colour ways when released.

Title: Great bounce!
Text: Look great, fit well, even on my wide feet. Excellent for speed training with what feels like fantastic energy return. Not the most stable though, I have to pay attention to the surface I'm running on having almost turned an ankle. Pros outweigh that con though and I am very happy with them.

Title: Very comfortable shoes for running.
Text: The shoes are very comfortable for long distance running for both long runs and tempo sessions. Their weight is perfect and the colour is gorgeous. True to size.

Title: Great shoe
Text: Great shoe for running/walking and looks awesome. Recommend

Title: Good shoe
Text: good shoe. bags a big around the sides and the tounge needs to be about 5-10mm longer.  need not to have these on constant auction only

Title: Style and performance in one
Text: A great balance between quality and price!  I would buy more on this line

Title: Excellent training shoe
Text: Love running in these. Light, comfortable and feel fast. As with other shoes using this foam, feel like they get even better after a few runs. Personally don’t find have any issues with instability.

Title: super comfy and amazing shoe
Text: Design of the tongue could be improved though, as it keeps slipping down during runs

Title: Great daily trainer, cushioning & bounce
Text: Bought these to replace my Boston 12 and I much prefer them. Also way more cushion than the Adizero SL which I’ve relegated to city walks. I’m marathon training and these have a great cushion and bounce to do the miles and tempo sessions. I use my pro3 for track and will do for the race. These are the perfect complement for daily training. My legs aren’t so sore after long runs. They look great too. They are also great for tempo and fartlek runs. I have narrow feet so these are perfect, but broader may find them tighter. Would buy again.

Title: mane tour mind uo Adidas, too many shoes
Text: not the best shoe they have made, a cheaper version of the pro 4. superblast 2 would be a better option

Title: Love them to run in but also great for walking
Text: Perfect city shoe, walking to work using after work for gym / a run

Title: Interesting daily trainer
Text: Initial impression after a short run: - MIDFOOT: foam is dense exactly like Adios Pro 3’s as expected, good enough grip with the Conti, no slipping and sliding on damp pavement with autumn leaves, but could’ve been grippier - FOREFOOT: feels very close to the ground, which I don’t love - HEEL: a little unstable especially at slower paces - UPPER: really don’t like the tongue, laces are thin and kinda annoying but do stay in place, material doesn’t feel premium, and could use a heel tab as putting them on is a bit fiddly - WIDTH: as someone who has normal width feet, this feels a bit narrow in the middle, with my feet bulging out a little to the side - RIDE: feels a lot better at faster efforts vs easy paces, but kinda miss having some stiffness - COMP: it’s not a comp for the Superblast 2 as initially thought, which still feels more premium and better all around (incl stability and grip) especially for long runs, probably closer to Rebel v4, Novablast 4 or Pegasus Plus - OVERALL: i do still really like it, would be interesting to see how it breaks in over time and looking forward to taking it out on some longer miles
